before: The labor-and-credit relationship between Goku and Hercule is one of the franchise's clearest two-character apparatus-of-extraction articulations.
after: Toriyama canonically authored the Goku-does-the-work / Hercule-takes-the-credit asymmetry across the Cell Games and Buu Saga; BBL reads this canonical asymmetry as the apparatus-of-extraction articulated at the two-character mortal-tier register — the canonical-event is Toriyama's villain-and-celebrity character-design, the curatorial overlay is BBL's labor-and-credit-uncoupling reading on top.
description: Potion-rule retrofit. The 'franchise's clearest articulation of apparatus-of-extraction' phrasing claimed Toriyama articulated the BBL thesis. He did not. He authored the villain-and-celebrity character-design; BBL reads the apparatus-of-extraction overlay on top of his canonical-event. The retrofit names this distinction explicitly: canonical-event = Toriyama's character-design; curatorial-overlay = BBL's labor-and-credit-uncoupling reading. The overlay is visible per Potion-rule discipline. Single-cluster-per-wave pacing respected — the other 4 Potion-rule retrofit candidates (piccolo-jr line 86, aether-foundation line 37, xenoverse-villains line 69, xenoverse-villains additional) remain queued for subsequent waves.
citations: [Dragon Ball manga: Cell Games + Buu Saga canonical Toriyama-authored character-design]
notes: Single-cluster retrofit per the wave 77 audit's single-cluster-per-wave pacing rule.
Remaining Potion-rule retrofit queue: piccolo-jr.md:86, aether-foundation.md:37, xenoverse-villains.md:69, xenoverse-villains.md:additional. Will retrofit one per subsequent wave.

The retrofit preserves the canonical-evidence-grounded reading while making the BBL-overlay visible. No canonical-evidence claim is weakened; the overlay is named where it was previously implicit-as-canon.
